metadata.title: States PCS: Himachal Pradesh GK for HPPSC Exams

questions.question: Which princely state in Himachal Pradesh was the first to sign the Instrument of Accession to India in 1947?
questions.explanation: Bilaspur was the first princely state in Himachal Pradesh to sign the Instrument of Accession in 1947, enabling swift integration into the Indian Union.

questions.question: In which year did Himachal Pradesh become a full-fledged state in the Indian Union?
questions.explanation: Himachal Pradesh became the 18th state of India on 25 January 1971.

questions.question: Which river forms the eastern boundary between Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand?
questions.explanation: The Tons River, a Yamuna tributary, marks the eastern boundary between Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand.

questions.question: Which is the largest district by area in Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Lahaul and Spiti is the largest district by area in Himachal Pradesh, known for its cold desert topography.

questions.question: In which district of Himachal Pradesh is the famous apple-growing region of Kotgarh located?
questions.explanation: Kotgarh is in Shimla district and is renowned as the birthplace of apple cultivation in Himachal Pradesh, introduced by Samuel Stokes.

questions.question: Which river is the longest to flow entirely within the state of Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Sutlej is the longest river traversing entirely through Himachal Pradesh before entering Punjab.

questions.question: Which valley in Himachal Pradesh is renowned for its Buddhist monasteries and strong Tibetan cultural influence?
questions.explanation: Spiti Valley, in the Lahaul and Spiti district, is known for its ancient Buddhist monasteries and vibrant Tibetan culture due to its proximity to Tibet.

questions.question: Which of the following biosphere reserves in Himachal Pradesh is included in UNESCO’s World Network of Biosphere Reserves?

options.label: C
options.text: Great Himalayan National Park Biosphere Reserve

questions.explanation: The Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve, covering parts of Lahaul, Spiti, and Kinnaur, is listed by UNESCO for its unique high-altitude desert ecosystem.

questions.question: Which of the following is the state tree of Himachal Pradesh and a prominent species in its temperate forests?
questions.explanation: Deodar (Cedrus deodara) is native to the Western Himalayas, forms extensive temperate forests, and is Himachal Pradesh’s state tree.

questions.question: Who was called Pahari Gandhi by Jawaharlal Nehru in 1937?
questions.explanation: Baba Kanshi Ram, born in Himachal Pradesh, was honored as Pahari Gandhi by Jawaharlal Nehru in 1937 for his freedom struggle activities. He vowed to wear black after Bhagat Singh’s execution and was also known as Siyahposh Jarnail.
